scoreChanges.headline: FORENSIC ANCHOR — Night 6. Third confirmed dual-trigger Gen-2 forensic anchor. Published 81.4 is the F500 Gen-2 4x7+3.5x1 cluster profile (ranks 10-17), identical to Masimo (April 23) and TIAA (April 23). Independent real-world validation: $20M compensatory verdict stands for Gary Walker, a retired truck driver who developed non-Hodgkin's lymphoma after 47 years of exposure to ethylene oxide emissions from BD's Covington, GA sterilization plant — residential-adjacent facility operating less than one mile from homes, schools, and a community baseball field. 402 pending cases in Georgia; one firm alone represents 272 plaintiffs. The $50M punitive phase was declared a mistrial after juror dissent on intent; the compensatory award stands. This is the sixth consecutive night confirming Gen-2 systematic overscoring across F500. Band change Exemplary to Functional.
evidence: $20M compensatory jury verdict stands (Gary Walker non-Hodgkin lymphoma vs. BD/Bard — ethylene oxide emissions from Covington GA plant). Punitive phase mistrial; compensatory award stands. Source: https://blog.cvn.com/becton-dickinson-hit-with-20m-verdict-in-toxic-chemical-and-cancer-trial
402 pending ethylene oxide exposure cases in Georgia against BD; 272 represented by a single plaintiff firm. Source: https://www.torhoermanlaw.com/ethylene-oxide-lawsuit/

Covington plant operated less than one mile from homes, schools, and community baseball field — residential-adjacent industrial harm. Source: https://topclassactions.com/lawsuit-settlements/lawsuit-news/ford-recalls-1-74-million-vehicles-due-to-rearview-camera-issues/

Published 81.4 is the F500 Gen-2 4x7+3.5x1 uniform cluster at ranks 10-17 — shared with American Express, Cummins, Danaher, Eli Lilly, General Mills, Masimo, US Bancorp. Third forensic anchor confirming the cluster is not evidence-based.

BD core healthcare mission (safety-engineered devices, global diagnostics access) supports functional-band floor; no SEC/DOJ securities action or criminal finding. Source: https://www.expertinstitute.com/resources/insights/20m-verdict-georgia-ethylene-oxide-cancer-trial/

scoreChanges.headline: Countries ceiling Night 4. Published 97.5 is a uniform 4.5x6+5x2=97.5 cluster artifact shared across top-rank countries. Luxembourg's evidence base is genuine (universal healthcare via CNS, tripartite social dialogue, EU FRA commendation) and the Exemplary band is defensible, but the specific composite is overstated. LuxLeaks (2014) tax-avoidance facilitation legacy is an I1 values-gap anchor; per-capita emissions among highest in EU due to cross-border tank tourism are an unresolved SYS/S4 contradiction. EU FRA 2025 report documents gaps in Roma integration and migrant-worker protections. GRETA 2024 flagged labor-trafficking in construction. Proposed 81.3 reflects an honest ceiling audit within the Exemplary band. Medium confidence — evidence base limited by secondary sourcing.
evidence: Universal healthcare via CNS mandatory insurance; 97% population coverage — EMP/E1-E2 anchor 1. Tripartite social dialogue institutionalized via Conseil economique et social.
EU FRA 2025 report: gaps in Roma integration, migrant-worker protections in construction and agriculture — EQU/EQ2 partial regression. Source: https://fra.europa.eu/en/country-data/2025/luxembourg

GRETA 2024 report: praised victim-support framework; flagged labor-trafficking prevalence in construction sector — BND/B2 mixed signal. Source: https://www.coe.int/en/web/anti-human-trafficking/luxembourg

LuxLeaks (2014) tax-avoidance facilitation legacy — I1 values-gap anchor; EU investigation confirmed systematic facilitation of corporate tax avoidance. INT 75.0 reflects partial resolution through UPR/treaty-body honest engagement.
Per-capita CO2 emissions among highest in EU due to cross-border tank tourism (fuel price arbitrage) — SYS/S4 unresolved contradiction for a country with otherwise strong sustainability rhetoric.

scoreChanges.headline: Countries ceiling Night 4. Published 100 is a methodology artifact — the uniform 4.5x6+5x2=100 profile is shared with Finland, Denmark, Norway, and Sweden, all of which have received evidence-based corrections. Iceland's institutional record is substantively exemplary (universal healthcare, CESCR 2024 commendation, disability-pension reform, national housing policy). Gaps exist for migrants, disabled populations, and trans communities per UN Independent Expert. High confidence ceiling correction within Exemplary band — this is not a governance failure finding but a methodology artifact correction.
evidence: Universal healthcare via Health Care Services Act; 6-month residency trigger — genuine EMP/E1 exemplary anchor. CESCR 2024 commendation on National Human Rights Institute establishment. Source: https://www.ohchr.org/en/news/2024/09/experts-committee-economic-social-and-cultural-rights-commend-icelands-move-establish

Disability-pension reform raising payments for over 95% of beneficiaries (OHCHR May 2025) — ACT/A4 positive. Source: https://www.ohchr.org/en/press-releases/2025/05/iceland-social-rights-and-safety-all-must-remain-top-priority-says-un-expert
National housing policy and action plan adopted 2024 — SYS/S1 anchor for structural investment in welfare infrastructure.

UN Independent Expert identified honest documented gaps for migrants, disabled, and trans populations — INT 93.8 held (below 100) reflecting partial self-disclosure of limitation. Source: https://2021-2025.state.gov/reports/2023-country-reports-on-human-rights-practices/iceland/

Countries-ceiling methodology artifact confirmed: uniform 4.5x6+5x2=100 profile documented across 5 Nordic entities (Finland, Denmark, Norway, Sweden, Iceland); Norway corrected April 22; Iceland is the fifth confirmed ceiling-artifact entity in the group.

scoreChanges.headline: US-States top-rank ceiling artifact — first surface, Night 1. Minnesota (rank 2, 95.9) is the second us-states entity to exhibit the same uniform-score ceiling pattern previously documented in countries (Norway/Germany/Netherlands Nights 2-3) and F500 Gen-2. Published 95.9 is a 4.5x7+4x1=95.9 uniform profile. Real evidence is substantive and broadly Exemplary: MinnesotaCare public option, expanded Human Rights Act enforcement, CROWN Act, Paid Family and Medical Leave (effective 2026), voting-rights restoration. Gaps: DOJ consent decree with Minneapolis PD (2023) acknowledging ongoing civil-rights issues; persistent racial-outcome disparities per MDH 2024-25; rural broadband coverage below 60% on Tribal lands; MMIW task force established but with documented efficacy gaps. Medium confidence — us-states evidence base is structurally thinner than countries.
evidence: MinnesotaCare public option operational since 1992; Paid Family and Medical Leave effective 2026; voting-rights restoration for returning citizens; driver's licenses for undocumented residents — genuine ACT/A1-A2 exemplary anchors. Source: https://mn.gov/boards/human-rights/

DOJ consent decree with Minneapolis Police Department (2023) acknowledges documented pattern of civil-rights violations — BND/B1 regression anchor; ACC/AB1 partial positive (consent decree is accountability signal). Source: https://www.justice.gov/opa/pr/justice-department-announces-consent-decree-city-minneapolis
Minnesota Department of Health 2024-25 data: persistent racial-outcome disparities across health, education, income — EQU/EQ2-EQ3 anchor limiting upper Exemplary sub-band placement.
Rural broadband coverage below 60% on Tribal lands — ACT/A4 and EQU/EQ4 gap for Indigenous communities.

Published 95.9 matches us-states Gen-2 4.5x7+4x1 uniform cluster; Minnesota is rank 2. First us-states entity in the ceiling-artifact pattern also documented in countries (Norway/Germany/Netherlands) and F500 (TIAA cluster). Source: https://www.auditor.leg.state.mn.us/

scoreChanges.headline: US-States top-rank ceiling artifact — first surface, Night 1. Vermont (rank 1, 97.5) leads the us-states index at a score produced by a 4.5x7+5x1=97.5 uniform profile. Vermont's institutional record is substantively strong: Act 48 universal healthcare framework, Act 46 Racial Equity Advisory Panel (statutory racial-equity infrastructure), lowest incarceration rate in New England, Act 250 environmental review as structural SYS protection, Act 173 disability-services reform (2024), highest per-pupil public education spending in the US with strong outcomes. Real gaps: rural access and broadband documented in 2024 VDH reports, 2024-25 housing affordability crisis with rising homelessness, and documented capacity limits on some equity programs. Medium confidence — this is a methodology artifact correction within the Exemplary band, not a governance failure finding.
evidence: Act 48 (2011) universal healthcare framework; strong Medicaid expansion; highest per-pupil public education cost in US with documented strong outcomes — EMP/E1 and ACT/A1 genuine exemplary anchors. Source: https://www.healthvermont.gov/

Act 46 Racial Equity Advisory Panel — statutory racial-equity infrastructure; Act 173 disability-services reform (2024) — EQU/EQ1 and EQU/EQ4 positive anchors. Source: https://racialequity.vermont.gov/
Lowest incarceration rate in New England; structured diversion and restorative justice programs — BND/B3 anchor for commitment to alternatives to punitive enforcement.
Act 250 environmental review framework — SYS/S3-S4 structural protection for land-use and environmental harm prevention. Source: https://legislature.vermont.gov/statutes/title/10/act-250

Published 97.5 matches us-states Gen-2 4.5x7+5x1 uniform cluster at rank 1. 2024-25 VDH reports document rural broadband and housing affordability gaps; these prevent perfect-ceiling placement. This is the first us-states ceiling-artifact correction — Vermont and Minnesota together mark the emergence of a third index with this pattern (after F500 Gen-2 and countries top-rank).

scoreChanges.headline: Rotation audit correction within Exemplary band. Published 95.9 slightly overstates the evidence-based position. Core record is genuinely strong: open-source model hub with documented community governance, transparent content-moderation and provenance disclosure, BigScience BLOOM collaborative-governance precedent, EU AI Act compliance infrastructure partnership. BND dimension drops to 75.0 reflecting consent and content-moderation tensions around adult and deepfake-adjacent material on the hub. INT dimension rises to 95.0 reflecting honest governance disclosure and cross-organizational safety collaboration. No in-window escalation in regulatory posture or documented harm. No band change. High confidence.
evidence: Open-source model hub with documented community governance and model card provenance disclosure — INT/I1-I2 exemplary anchors. Source: https://huggingface.co/
BigScience BLOOM multi-organization collaborative-governance precedent — SYS/S2 exemplary anchor demonstrating scalable open-science infrastructure. Source: https://huggingface.co/blog/bigscience
EU AI Act compliance infrastructure partner — ACC/AB4 positive signal for proactive regulatory engagement.
BND 75.0 reflects documented consent and content-moderation tensions around adult-content and deepfake-adjacent material hosted on the platform — not resolved in the 14-day window.
No new regulatory action, enforcement finding, or harm event in the 14-day window. Correction is methodology audit within band, not event-driven.

confirmations.headline: Illinois vote deferral hold. Both SB 3261 (Anthropic-backed) and SB 3444 (OpenAI-backed) committee deadlines moved to May 15, 2026 via Rule 2-10. No substantive committee vote held April 24. No new material to shift score. Composite held at 61.6 — 1.6 points above the Established/Functional boundary. Next vote window is May 15; reassessment is mandatory at that date regardless of outcome.

Illinois vote deferral hold. SB 3444 Rule 2-10 deadline moved to May 15, 2026 — no committee vote held April 24. Proposed score 27.5 applied April 23 stands as current. Musk v. Altman trial jury selection begins April 27; score will require reassessment during trial week. Next vote window May 15.

Critical floor maintained. Musk snubbed Paris prosecutors April 20; French prosecutors alerted SEC and DOJ to potential IPO market manipulation tied to Grok deepfake controversy. Additional financial-fraud dimension noted but index floor limits downward expression of additional negative evidence. Score confirmed at 2.2. Full evidence cluster (CSAM class-action, 37-AG response, EU proceeding, UK ICO investigation) unchanged.

Reaffirmation. Live countries.json holds DRC at composite 4.4 (applied 2026-04-22). The assessor produced a change proposal from a stale 10.9 baseline, but the proposed 4.4 matches the live score exactly. This assessment re-grounds the April 22 correction against fresh April 2026 methodology audit: M23 immunity demand blocking all accountability progress, 500K+ newly displaced in South Kivu since December 2025, UN Security Council emergency session April 22, collapsed ceasefire with active atrocity documentation. No score movement — reaffirmation of the applied 4.4 Critical position.

Deferred — UNMISS mandate renewal pending. Security Council vote expected before April 30. Active hostilities between SSPDF and SPLA-IO at worst scale since 2018 peace agreement. Score held at 0.0 Critical pending vote outcome. Mandate lapse or modification would trigger immediate reassessment.

Critical floor confirmed; earthquake scan flag noted with date-verification caveat. April 24 scanner flagged a 7.7-magnitude earthquake in central Myanmar but confirmed reporting from Reuters/AP/UN OCHA was not retrieved before assessment close. Assessor instructs: verify earthquake date before scoring humanitarian response dimension. SAC-M/ANFREL joint report (April 10) documenting illegitimate sham elections and junta atrocities at 5-year high post-coup stands as the in-window anchor. Score confirmed at 3.1 Critical from April 23 assessment.

Confirmed within threshold. EU GDPR fine of approximately 200M euros (week of April 20) for AI training data scraping without adequate consent is new material since April 23 scan close. Combined with the March 2026 New Mexico $375M verdict and Senate investigation, Meta faces three-track governmental enforcement. Delta within confirmation threshold; published score stands. EU fine adds directional weight for next full assessment.

sectorAlerts.sector: AI Labs — Illinois Legislation Vote Deferral
sectorAlerts.alert: Both SB 3261 (Anthropic-backed: mandatory safety plans, child-harm liability) and SB 3444 (OpenAI-backed: mass-casualty liability shield including CBRN scenarios) had their committee deadlines moved to May 15, 2026 via Rule 2-10 Committee/3rd Reading Deadline. No substantive committee vote was held April 24. Anthropic held at 61.6 (1.6pts above Established/Functional boundary); OpenAI held at 27.5. Both are confirmations this cycle. The next vote window is May 15 — mandatory reassessment for both entities at that date.

sectorAlerts.sector: AI Labs — xAI/Grok Financial Fraud Dimension
sectorAlerts.alert: Musk failed to appear before Paris prosecutors on April 20 (criminal court non-appearance). French prosecutors alerted SEC and DOJ to potential IPO market manipulation: allegation that the Grok deepfake controversy was deliberately orchestrated to inflate the value of X and xAI ahead of a planned June 2026 SpaceX/xAI merger IPO. This adds a financial-fraud dimension to the existing CSAM/deepfake case cluster. xAI/Grok is confirmed at the index floor (2.2 Critical); the composite cannot express additional downward evidence at this level. Full floor-limitation methodology note applies.

sectorAlerts.sector: Fortune 500 — Generation-2 Night 6 (Becton Dickinson Forensic Anchor)
sectorAlerts.alert: Becton Dickinson is the third consecutive dual-trigger Gen-2 forensic anchor, completing the pattern established by TIAA (April 23) and Masimo (April 23). The 4x7+3.5x1=81.4 cluster at F500 ranks 10-17 is now invalidated by three independent anchors with independent real-world harm evidence. Six consecutive nights of confirmed systematic overscoring. Remaining unassessed cluster entities (American Express, Cummins, Danaher, Eli Lilly, General Mills, US Bancorp) are high-probability artifacts. The April 23 sprint recommendation remains open and is now urgent — six nights of confirmation without sprint action increases external discovery risk.

sectorAlerts.sector: US-States Index — Top-Rank Ceiling Artifact (First Surface)
sectorAlerts.alert: Vermont (rank 1, 97.5) and Minnesota (rank 2, 95.9) are both first-baseline assessments showing the same uniform-score ceiling pattern previously documented in countries (Norway/Germany/Netherlands, Nights 2-3) and F500 Gen-2 (Nights 1-6). Both corrections are medium-confidence at -10.0 and -11.5 respectively; both remain in Exemplary band. This is the emergence of a THIRD index ceiling-artifact pattern. Recommend auditing us-states ranks 3-10 before the next index build to confirm the pattern extent.

Luxembourg (97.5 → 81.3, -16.2) and Iceland (100 → 87.5, -12.5) continue the countries-index top-rank ceiling pattern. Norway corrected April 22 (-15.3); Germany and Netherlands corrected April 23 (-23.1 and -21.5). Five of the top 10 countries by published score have now received evidence-based corrections over three nights. Denmark (100), Sweden (97.2), and Finland (100) remain unassessed at ceiling-level composites; all are T2 methodology-sweep candidates.

sectorAlerts.sector: Scanner Quality — Date-Drift and Unverified Source Flags
sectorAlerts.alert: Two scanner quality issues flagged for prompt refinement: (1) Myanmar earthquake April 24 — scan news_summary included this event but confirmed source URLs were not available at scan close; assessor instructed to retrieve Reuters/AP/UN OCHA reporting before scoring but earthquake date requires independent confirmation. (2) Meta EU GDPR fine of approximately 200M euros — cited in assessor summary but primary source (EDPB enforcement decision) was not retrieved; secondary press coverage only. Recommend scanner prompt refinement to require primary-source retrieval for: (a) same-day acute events; (b) regulatory enforcement actions above 50M euros.

insights: Tonight's Becton Dickinson anchor completes a six-night record of confirmed F500 Gen-2 systematic overscoring. The pattern is no longer a batch-level hypothesis — it is a documented, multi-anchor, cross-night finding. Three forensic anchors (TIAA, Masimo, Becton Dickinson) have independently validated the 4x7+3.5x1=81.4 cluster. The remaining six entities in that cluster are not candidates for possible correction; they are near-certainties. The sprint recommendation from April 23 is now overdue.

The Illinois SB 3261/SB 3444 vote deferral to May 15 is analytically significant. The benchmark held both Anthropic and OpenAI at their April 23 positions rather than applying speculative outcomes. This discipline — hold on deferral, not on anticipation — is the correct application of the assessment protocol. The 1.6-point Anthropic margin above the Established/Functional boundary now extends to May 15 without change. The risk is unchanged; the timeline is extended.

Vermont and Minnesota mark the first surface of a us-states ceiling-artifact pattern. This is structurally identical to the countries-index ceiling discovered on April 22, and the F500 Gen-2 ceiling discovered on April 19. Three separate indexes now exhibit the same upward clustering bias at the top of their respective rank distributions. The pattern is consistent with a single root cause: published scores in all three indexes were generated with insufficient differentiation at the upper bound. The index build process requires an evidence-of-excellence gate before any future entity receives a score above 90.

The DRC reaffirmation illustrates an important pipeline integrity case. The change-proposal file carried a stale published composite of 10.9, while the live countries.json already reflects the 4.4 correction applied April 22. The assessor's proposed 4.4 matched the live score exactly, producing a net-zero delta — confirming the April 22 correction rather than generating a new change. The fresh April 2026 evidence (M23 immunity demand, 500K+ newly displaced, UN Security Council emergency session, collapsed ceasefire) re-grounds the 4.4 position against current methodology. This is a reaffirmation, not a correction.

Hugging Face at 88.1 is the highest ai-labs composite after tonight's rotation audit, confirming its position as the top-ranked ai-labs entity by an evidence-based assessment. The 7.8-point correction is not a governance failure finding — it is a ceiling audit removing the residual uniform-profile premium from the original published 95.9. The INT dimension rises to 95.0 on honest governance disclosure, which is counter-intuitive but correct: the correction process itself demonstrates the transparency the INT dimension rewards.

ACC remains the universal downward pressure dimension across all six nights and all three indexes. No entity in any sector has generated voluntary upward ACC movement. Every positive ACC signal in the record is externally forced — by court verdict, regulatory action, legislative mandate, or treaty body finding. The benchmark's most durable cross-sectoral analytical claim is that accountability is structurally reactive, not proactive, at the institutional level. Tonight's six proposals are fully consistent with this pattern.
highlights: Becton Dickinson -27.3 (81.4 → 54.1, Exemplary → Functional): third Gen-2 forensic anchor in two nights. $20M cancer verdict for ethylene oxide exposure, 402 pending cases, residential-adjacent industrial harm cluster. Six-night F500 Gen-2 pattern confirmed.

Illinois SB 3261 and SB 3444 both deferred to May 15 via Rule 2-10. Anthropic held at 61.6 (1.6pts above Established/Functional boundary); OpenAI held at 27.5. Both are cycle confirmations; next vote window is May 15.

US-States ceiling artifact — first surface. Vermont (rank 1, 97.5 → 87.5) and Minnesota (rank 2, 95.9 → 84.4) both exhibit the same uniform-score ceiling pattern documented in countries (Nights 2-3) and F500 (Nights 1-6). Third index confirmed with this structural bias.

Countries ceiling Night 4: Luxembourg (97.5 → 81.3, -16.2) and Iceland (100 → 87.5, -12.5). Five of the top 10 countries by published score have now received evidence-based corrections over three nights.

xAI/Grok floor limitation confirmed. Musk criminal court non-appearance April 20; SEC/DOJ alerted to potential IPO market manipulation. Index floor (2.2) limits downward expression of additional negative evidence.

DRC reaffirmation: live score 4.4 Critical confirmed against fresh April 2026 methodology audit. Assessor proposal was from stale 10.9 baseline; proposed 4.4 matched live — net-zero delta, reaffirmation not correction.

Hugging Face 95.9 → 88.1 (-7.8): rotation audit within Exemplary band. No governance failure. Remains highest ai-labs composite after evidence-based ceiling audit. INT rises to 95.0 on honest governance disclosure.

sectorTrends.trend: Six consecutive nights of F500 Gen-2 systematic overscoring confirmed. Three forensic anchors produced over two nights: TIAA (-38.9), Masimo (-33.0), Becton Dickinson (-27.3). The 4x7+3.5x1=81.4 cluster is fully invalidated at ranks 10-17. Remaining unassessed cluster entities (American Express, Cummins, Danaher, Eli Lilly, General Mills, US Bancorp) are high-probability artifacts. Six nights of pattern confirmation without a coordinated sprint is the single largest unresolved risk to index integrity. ACC is the most consistently overstated dimension; SYS shows genuine positive variation even within high-delta entities (Becton Dickinson SYS 62.5 assessed, reflecting real healthcare mission).

sectorTrends.sector: AI Labs / Governance Timeline and Accountability Stress
sectorTrends.trend: Illinois legislative deferral extends the Anthropic/OpenAI governance inflection point to May 15. The Musk v. Altman trial opens April 27 — four weeks of potential Tier-5 evidence entering the public record. xAI/Grok adds a financial-fraud dimension to its existing CSAM cluster, but the index floor limits expression. Hugging Face confirms its position as the highest-scoring evidence-based ai-labs entity at 88.1. The ai-labs index now shows a 85.9-point span from Hugging Face (88.1 Exemplary) to xAI/Grok (2.2 Critical) — the widest confirmed evidence-based range of any single-sector index in the benchmark.

sectorTrends.sector: Countries / Ceiling Audit and Active Humanitarian Cluster
sectorTrends.trend: Night 4 of countries ceiling correction: Luxembourg (-16.2) and Iceland (-12.5) follow Norway (-15.3, April 22) and Germany/Netherlands (-23.1/-21.5, April 23). Five top-10 countries corrected in three nights; Denmark, Sweden, and Finland remain at ceiling-level published composites without evidence-based baselines. The mid-range countries (Established and upper Functional) remain the least-assessed segment. Four concurrent critical-band humanitarian crises (DRC, South Sudan, Myanmar, Israel/Gaza) continue without improvement signal across any assessed dimension.

sectorTrends.sector: US States / First Ceiling Artifact Detection
sectorTrends.trend: Vermont and Minnesota are the first us-states entities assessed under evidence-based methodology, and both immediately exhibit the ceiling-artifact pattern. This is the third index in which the top-rank cluster shows systematic uniform-score inflation. The pattern extends the benchmark's core methodology finding — published indexes across fortune-500, countries, and us-states all over-represent institutional quality at the top of their rank distributions — to a third index in a single week of pipeline operation.

emergingRisks: April 25: Federal-contractor DEI EO 14398 certification deadline. IBM $17M FCA settlement (April 10) is the enforcement template. F500 entities with substantial federal contracting exposure (Lockheed Martin, Raytheon, Northrop Grumman, Booz Allen Hamilton, Leidos, DXC) should be queued for T1 assessment from April 25 onward if new settlements or EEOC letters are disclosed.

April 27: Musk v. Altman trial opens in Oakland federal court. Four-week docket of testimony from Altman, Brockman, and Satya Nadella. Brockman diary and Zilis 2018 texts are already in evidence. Reassess OpenAI at minimum weekly through May 25. Score currently 27.5 Developing.

April 30: UNMISS mandate expiry. Security Council vote expected before April 30. South Sudan deferred pending vote outcome. Mandate lapse or modified mandate would trigger immediate reassessment from 0.0 Critical baseline.

May 15: Illinois SB 3261 and SB 3444 Rule 2-10 deadline. Mandatory reassessment for Anthropic (61.6, 1.6pts above Established/Functional boundary) and OpenAI (27.5 Developing) regardless of vote outcome.
May 19: Anthropic v. DoD oral arguments in DC. Pentagon blacklisting and supply-chain-risk designation; material for INT, SYS, and ACC dimensions.
US-States ranks 3-10 ceiling audit required before next index build. Vermont and Minnesota both exhibit ceiling artifact as ranks 1-2; ranks 3-10 are high-probability ceiling candidates.
Countries ranks 1-10 remaining ceiling audit: Denmark (100), Sweden (97.2), and Finland (100) are unassessed T2 candidates following five top-10 corrections in three nights.

F500 Gen-2 sprint: six unassessed entities in 4x7+3.5x1=81.4 cluster (American Express, Cummins, Danaher, Eli Lilly, General Mills, US Bancorp) plus 30+ entities in 3.5x8 and 3.5x6+3.0x2 clusters. Six nights without sprint action is the current unresolved methodology risk.
